sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Girona FC has had mixed results in their last 5 matches with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses, showing inconsistency. Atlético Madrid, on the other hand, is in strong form with 3 wins, 1 draw, and 1 loss, including high-scoring victories. Their recent performances suggest Atlético holds an edge in confidence and attacking prowess.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head matches, Atlético Madrid has dominated Girona FC, winning both games with scores of 3-0 and 3-1. This historical advantage, combined with Atlético's tendency to score first in 6 of the last 8 encounters, points to their superiority in direct matchups.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Girona FC struggles defensively, conceding 56 goals this season (16th in the league), while Atlético Madrid has a robust defense, conceding only 30 goals (among the best). Offensively, Atlético has scored 64 goals compared to Girona's 44, highlighting their greater firepower and balance.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Injuries
sections.text: Girona misses key players like Miguel Gutiérrez, impacting their defense, while Atlético has no major absences and in-form forwards like Alexander Sørloth (5 goals in last 5) and Julián Álvarez (3 goals). This gives Atlético a significant advantage in attack.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Girona's high possession (56.8%, 3rd in league) might be neutralized by Atlético's effective counter-attacks and defensive structure. Atlético's ability to score late (26.7% of goals after 76 minutes) could exploit Girona's defensive lapses in crucial moments.
